types.name: INTEGER
types.description: Signed 4-byte integer. Range: -2147483648 to 2147483647.

types.name: SMALLINT
types.description: Signed 2-byte integer. Range: -32768 to 32767.

types.name: SERIAL
types.description: Auto-incrementing 4-byte integer (not null, sequence-backed). Equivalent to INTEGER with a DEFAULT nextval(seq).
types.notes: Prefer GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY in PG 10+.

types.name: BIGSERIAL
types.description: Auto-incrementing 8-byte integer.

types.name: SMALLSERIAL
types.description: Auto-incrementing 2-byte integer.

types.name: NUMERIC
types.description: Exact numeric with user-specified precision and scale. Arbitrary precision.

types.name: REAL
types.description: 4-byte single-precision floating-point number (approx 6 decimal digits of precision).

types.name: DOUBLE PRECISION
types.description: 8-byte double-precision floating-point number (approx 15 decimal digits of precision).

types.name: MONEY
types.description: Currency amount. Fraction precision depends on lc_monetary. Range: -92233720368547758.08 to +92233720368547758.07.
types.notes: Use NUMERIC for portable currency storage.

types.name: VARCHAR
types.description: Variable-length string with limit n. If n is omitted, any length is accepted.

types.name: TEXT
types.description: Variable unlimited-length string. Preferred over VARCHAR without a limit in PostgreSQL.

types.name: CHAR
types.description: Fixed-length, blank-padded string of exactly n characters.

types.name: BYTEA
types.description: Binary string (byte array). Stores raw bytes.

types.name: TIMESTAMP
types.description: Date and time without timezone. Range: 4713 BC – 294276 AD. p = fractional seconds precision (0-6).

types.name: TIMESTAMPTZ
types.description: Date and time with timezone. Stored as UTC internally. p = fractional seconds precision (0-6).

types.name: DATE
types.description: Calendar date (year, month, day). Range: 4713 BC – 5874897 AD.

types.name: TIME
types.description: Time of day without timezone. Range: 00:00:00 – 24:00:00.

types.name: TIMETZ
types.description: Time of day with timezone.
types.notes: Use TIMESTAMPTZ in most cases instead.

types.name: INTERVAL
types.description: Time span. e.g., '1 year 2 months 3 days'. Supports optional field restriction and precision.

types.name: BOOLEAN
types.description: Logical boolean. Stores TRUE, FALSE, or NULL.

types.name: JSONB
types.description: JSON data stored in a decomposed binary format. Supports GIN indexes. Preferred over JSON for querying.

types.name: JSON
types.description: JSON data stored as exact text copy. Faster to input; use JSONB for querying/indexing.

types.name: UUID
types.description: Universally unique identifier (128-bit). Stored efficiently. Use gen_random_uuid() or uuid_generate_v4() for defaults.

types.name: INET
types.description: IPv4 or IPv6 host address (and optionally its subnet). Stores network mask with address.

types.name: CIDR
types.description: IPv4 or IPv6 network specification. Host bits must be zero.

types.name: MACADDR
types.description: MAC address (6 bytes).

types.name: MACADDR8
types.description: MAC address in EUI-64 format (8 bytes).

types.name: TEXT[]
types.description: Array of text. Any base type can be made into an array with [].

types.name: INTEGER[]
types.description: Array of integers.

types.name: JSONB[]
types.description: Array of JSONB values.

types.name: INT4RANGE
types.description: Range of integer.

types.name: INT8RANGE
types.description: Range of bigint.

types.name: NUMRANGE
types.description: Range of numeric.

types.name: TSRANGE
types.description: Range of timestamp without time zone.

types.name: TSTZRANGE
types.description: Range of timestamp with time zone.

types.name: DATERANGE
types.description: Range of date.

types.name: TSVECTOR
types.description: Sorted list of distinct lexemes. Used for full-text search.

types.name: TSQUERY
types.description: Text search query. Stores lexemes with operators &, |, !, <->.

types.name: POINT
types.description: Geometric point (x, y).

types.name: LINE
types.description: Infinite line defined by A, B, C where Ax + By + C = 0.

types.name: POLYGON
types.description: Closed geometric path (polygon).

types.name: BOX
types.description: Rectangular box defined by two corner points.

types.name: CIRCLE
types.description: Geometric circle (center point and radius).

types.name: XML
types.description: XML data. Stores well-formed XML.

types.name: OID
types.description: Object identifier. Used internally by PostgreSQL for system tables.

types.name: PG_LSN
types.description: PostgreSQL Log Sequence Number (WAL position).
